data.title: Zoom rises after appointing former Microsoft exec as chief product officer
data.description:
Shares of Zoom Communications (ZM) jumped about 4% on Wednesday after the company said it appointed Russell Dicker as chief product officer to advance its AI-first product strategy and workflow automation.

The company noted that Dicker brings more than 25 years of experience building and scaling global product platforms, including leading Microsoft (MSFT) Teams' product and data science teams, with a focus on applying AI to simplify workflows and improve how work gets done.

The company noted that Dicker will play a central role in advancing Zoom's AI strategy — overseeing the integration of AI and machine learning across the product portfolio.

Before joining Zoom, Russell served as corporate vice president of Product at Microsoft, where he led the product management and data science organizations for Microsoft Teams. Previously, he served as senior director of Product at Google Maps (GOOG) (GOOGL) and spent 15 years in product and engineering leadership roles at Amazon (AMZN).

data.title: Small-cap real estate stocks with A grade EPS revisions as earnings season begins
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season gets underway, the real estate sector is entering reporting season on a relative high, as investors sought defensive, income-generating exposure amid broader market volatility.

Within the small-cap REIT space, however, positive analyst earnings revisions remain selective, making the names that have earned them a particularly meaningful subset heading into Q1 reports.

The following list highlights small-cap real estate stocks that have earned a Seeking Alpha EPS Revision Quant Grade of A- or higher, spanning Hotel & Resort REITs, Office REITs, Health Care REITs, Diversified REITs, and Farmland. 

Leaders include Chatham Lodging Trust (CLDT), Farmland Partners Inc. (FPI), Alpine Income Property Trust, Inc. (PINE), Peakstone Realty Trust (PKST), Postal Realty Trust, Inc. (PSTL), and Sila Realty Trust, Inc. (SILA).

The list represents considerable diversity within real estate sub-sectors, spanning hotel & resort REITs, office REITs, healthcare REITs, diversified REITs, and multi-family residential REITs. These high EPS revision grades indicate positive sentiment from analysts regarding the companies’ future earnings potential.

  1. Chatham Lodging Trust (CLDT) – EPS revision grade: A+

  2. Farmland Partners Inc. (FPI) – EPS revision grade: A+

  3. Alpine Income Property Trust, Inc. (PINE) – EPS revision grade: A+

  4. Peakstone Realty Trust (PKST) – EPS revision grade: A+

  5. Postal Realty Trust, Inc. (PSTL) – EPS revision grade: A+

  6. Sila Realty Trust, Inc. (SILA) – EPS revision grade: A+

  7. American Assets Trust, Inc. (AAT) – EPS revision grade: A

  8. Veris Residential, Inc. (VRE) – EPS revision grade: A

  9. Xenia Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (XHR) – EPS revision grade: A

  10. Diversified Healthcare Trust (DHC) – EPS revision grade: A-

  11. JBG SMITH Properties (JBGS) – EPS revision grade: A-

  12. The RMR Group Inc. (RMR) – EPS revision grade: A-

Real Estate ETFs: (VNQ), (XLRE), (IYR), (USRT), and (HOMZ)

data.title: ReNew Energy reports record 2.4 GW additions, now India’s No. 2 operator
data.description:
ReNew Energy (RNW) up 1.7% in Wednesday's trading after saying it commissioned ~2.4 GW of new renewable assets in FY 2026, its highest-ever addition, lifting its total operating capacity to 12.6 GW as of March 31, making it the second-largest renewable energy portfolio in India, after adjusting for 600 MW of assets sold during the year.

ReNew (RNW) said the new capacity includes 1.75 GW of solar, 0.62 GW of wind, and 25 MW/100 MWh of battery energy storage systems; the company also has ~450 MW of fully constructed projects expected to be commissioned soon, and a gross portfolio of ~20 GW, including 1.7 GW of storage, making it one of the world's largest clean energy platforms.

The company's ReNew Green commercial and industrial arm has 2.5 GW of committed capacity and attracted a $95M equity investment in March, while the solar manufacturing business received a separate $100M equity injection earlier in the year.

The company said ReNew Green has a portfolio of 2.5 GW of committed capacity, of which more than 2 GW has been commissioned, and its solar manufacturing has 6.5 GW of operating module and 2.5 GW of cell capacity, which is being expanded by 4 GW by December 2026.

data.title: Alector gains as Cantor Fitzgerald upgrades on brain carrier platform
data.description:
• Alector (ALEC) shares rose on Wednesday after Cantor Fitzgerald upgraded the biotech to Overweight from Neutral, citing the potential in its blood-brain barrier ((BBB)) technology platform, Alector Brain Carrier ((ABC)).
  • In October, following a trial setback for a GSK (GSK)-partnered dementia therapy, San Francisco, California-based Alector (ALEC) decided to prioritize its resources in ABC, which is designed to support the delivery of brain-targeting therapies.
  • Following a discussion with top Alector (ALEC) executives, including CEO Arnon Rosenthal and medical chief Giacomo Salvadore, Cantor analyst Pete Stavropoulos said that the ABC platform has "significant potential value."
  • “The ABC platform positions ALEC to become a major player in the neurodegenerative space for drugs that leverage BBB-crossing tech to enhance delivery to the brain,” he wrote, adding that the value of the ABC platform is not adequately reflected in Alector (ALEC) shares currently.

data.title: Robinhood, Webull, eToro shares jump after SEC removes day-trading limit
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD) stock jumped 7.8%, Webull (BULL) stock climbed 8.9%, and eToro Group (ETOR) stock rose 5.2% in Wednesday morning trading after the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission approved rule changes that will make it easier for small investors to day trade.

The rule change, proposed by the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA), would eliminate the requirement that any "pattern day trader" would be required to keep at least $25,000 in a margin account before the customer can continue day trading.

A pattern day trader is defined as a customer who executes four or more day trades within five business days.

Instead, the revised rule requires that customers maintain equity in their margin account that's commensurate with the amount of market exposure they have at any given time of the trading day.

The SEC approved the rule change on an accelerated basis, it said.

Coinbase Global (COIN), which  has added stock and ETF trading, rose 3.8%.

Shares in Morgan Stanley (MS), which owns E*Trade and posted strong Q1 earnings on Wednesday, gained 5.1%. Charles Schwab (SCHW) stock rose 1.1%, Interactive Brokers (IBKR) climbed 2.6%,

data.title: Investors can expect ‘a muted Q2’ in big tech names due to ad budget cuts – analyst
data.description:
Mark Mahaney, Evercore ISI’s head of internet research, is warning investors to expect a muted second quarter from big tech companies as advertising budgets face cuts amid economic uncertainty.

In an interview with CNBC, Mahaney said his channel checks show that Q2 budgets are “starting to get cut,” creating headwinds for companies that rely heavily on advertising revenue.

“It’s going to be a problem for companies with exposure to brand advertising and maybe to performance marketing,” Mahaney said.

The analyst compared the current environment to the pullback seen three years ago following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, when ad budgets were trimmed for a quarter or two. He noted that brand advertising typically gets cut first when companies pull back on spending.

Despite the near-term caution, Mahaney identified Amazon (AMZN) and Meta Platforms (META) as what he calls “dislocated high quality” assets that are currently trading near trough multiples. These two companies have become top picks for the analyst because of their attractive valuations.

“When you find these high-quality assets that get dislocated, the narrative can turn,” he said.

Mahaney remains particularly bullish on AI initiatives at these companies, pointing to a significant narrative shift around Amazon (AMZN).

“Amazon, I think, is the Google of 2026,” he said, noting that the company may no longer be viewed as an AI laggard following revelations about its internal chip training capabilities.

The stock has already rallied 20% on what he calls the “mythos rally.”

The analyst acknowledged that tech companies have shifted from capital-light businesses to more capital-intensive models—what he describes as the “industrialization of tech.”

However, he believes this is a typical investment cycle rather than a permanent change, predicting that CapEx intensity will eventually moderate.

“I think the opportunity is just dramatically bigger… I think you’re going to get back to high ROIC businesses,” Mahaney said.

While companies may be hesitant when discussing their June outlooks, Mahaney believes investors should still consider buying into these high-quality names. He expects the current investment cycle to play out over the next three to five years, ultimately delivering strong returns for patient investors.

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT).

data.title: ASML raises 2026 sales outlook as AI demand boosts growth
data.description:
Chip equipment maker ASML (ASML) raised its full-year sales forecast, driven by AI infrastructure investments fueling semiconductor production that has boosted demand for its chipmaking machines.

Shares of ASML fell about 4% on Wednesday.

Key Metrics

ASML — which makes extreme ultraviolet lithography, or EUV, and Deep Ultraviolet, or DUV, lithography system — saw first-quarter net sales increase about 13.3% year-over-year to €8.77B, beating estimates.

"The semiconductor industry's growth outlook continues to solidify, driven by ongoing AI-related infrastructure investments. Demand for chips is outpacing supply. In response, our customers are accelerating their capacity expansion plans for 2026 and beyond, supported by long-term agreements with their customers. In the past months, our customers have increased their expected short- and medium-term demand for our products. ASML's order intake continues to be very strong as a result, and we are closely aligned with our customers to support their demand in a combination of delivery of new systems and performance upgrades of their installed base," said ASML's CEO Christophe Fouquet.

ASML noted last year that it would stop reporting order bookings after 2025, a key indicator of customer demand. The company said then that on an annual basis it will provide the total backlog.

Addressing ASML's ability to keep up with demand, CFO Roger Dassen said the company should be able to ship at least 60 systems for Low NA EUV in 2026. Dassen added that the company can get to at least 80 Low NA EUV units in 2027.

Fouquet noted that there is a lot of demand for advanced Memory and advanced Logic driven by investments in AI infrastructure. "We expect in fact that the supply will not meet the demand for the foreseeable future. So, this is creating a strong constraint in the end markets from AI to mobile and PC," said Fouquet. He added that "if we look at Memory, what our customers tell us is that they are sold out for 2026. And their supply constraints will last beyond 2026. For advanced Logic, we see our customers building capacity for several nodes, while they also continue to ramp 2 nm in order to address the AI products.

ASML's customers include leading chip manufacturers, such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Intel (INTC).

China Sales

China accounted for 19% of the total net system sales of about €6.3B in the first quarter of 2026. South Korea led with a 45% share, followed by Taiwan at 23%. The U.S. accounted for 12% of the total net system sales. In the fourth quarter of 2025, China accounted for 36% of the total net system sales of about €7.58B, followed by South Korea.

Outlook

"These business dynamics underpin our expectation that 2026 will be another growth year for all our businesses," said Fouquet. 

Fouquet noted that the company sees its Memory and Logic customers increasing their capital expenditure and trying to accelerate their capacity ramp in 2026 and beyond. "So this translates basically into higher lithointensity and a higher litho demand for ASML. So we're going to continue to work very closely with our customers to increase our capacity. We are doing that in 2026. We will continue to do that in 2027," Fouquet added.

For the second quarter of 2026, ASML expects total net sales to be between €8.4B and €9B with a gross margin between 51% and 52%.

For the full year 2026, the company now expects total net sales to be between €36B and €40B (midpoint at €38B), compared to the prior forecast of €34B to €39B (midpoint at €36.5B) provided in January with the fourth quarter results. ASML expects full-year gross margin between 51% and 53%.

SA Analyst Commentary

ASML’s Q1 sales showed sales that were near the high end of the guided €8.2-€8.8 billion range with gross margins meeting the high end of the range. More interesting was that the company increased its revenue guidance from €34-€39 billion to €36-€40 billion for 2026. That is a strong sign as it confirms the robustness of demand, but also that even with a tight supply chain ASML has found some space to generate €1-€2 billion higher sales this year than initially targeted and that includes export control headwinds. So, that likely also points at some improvement in ASML’s own supply chain, which I believe is a positive forward indicator for future years as well," said Seeking Alpha analyst Dhierin Bechai, Investing Group Leader for The Aerospace Forum.

Bechai added that "demand remains robust and ASML is now showing that it can deliver more than it initially expected. While that is a positive, we note that analysts were already expecting revenues near the mid-point of the new range. The new guidance supports that." 

Wall Street Analysts

JPMorgan analyst Sandeep Deshpande noted that ASML raising guidance at the first quarter rather than half-year shows the company’s confidence in demand strength, Bloomberg reported. ASML’s positive outlook with indications of “very strong” orders reads positively for other chip equipment companies, the analyst added.

Lee Simpson, analyst at Morgan Stanley, said that despite no mention of bookings, the company talked of demand outstripping supply and customers looking to accelerate capacity expansion, which suggests strong sales momentum into 2027. Simpson added that the guidance for at least 80 low-NA EUV tools available next year is positive, and that number could still rise on any determined buildout of clean room space available.

Analysts at Berenberg said that the guidance raise is “kind of” expected by the Street, although that signals the company’s confidence given it’s just the first quarter. Focus is mainly on 2027 at this point, and end-market commentary from the company continues to be solid, the analysts added.

"ASML provided a decent beat in its latest results, but nothing exceptional for what is a high-quality company with an enviable market position," said Ben Barringer, head of technology research at Quilter Cheviot. The analyst noted that the guidance raise "brings it more in line with where the market expects it to be, but a positive move nonetheless."

Dividend and Buyback

ASML said it intends to declare a total dividend for the year 2025 of €7.50 per ordinary share, which is a 17% increase compared to 2024. Recognizing the three interim dividends of €1.60 per ordinary share paid in 2025 and 2026, this leads to a final dividend proposal to the Annual General Meeting of €2.70 per ordinary share.

ASML noted that in the first quarter, it bought back about €1.1B worth of shares under the 2026–2028 share buyback program. The company intends to buy back shares worth up to €12B under this program.

Shares of other chip equipment makers on Wednesday. KLA (KLAC) slumped about 4%, while Lam Research (LRCX) fell around 3%. Applied Materials (AMAT) dipped about 2% on Wednesday.

Update: The story was updated with stock price movement.

data.title: Papa John's and Pizza Hut continue down the privatization path
data.description:
M&A activity around Papa John's International (PZZA) and Pizza Hut continues, according to Reuters.

Sources confirmed that Qatari-backed investment fund Irth Capital has been in talks with Papa John's (PZZA) and conducting due diligence on a potential acquisition over the last month. The talks have advanced to the point that a deal could be announced within weeks.

Elsewhere, bidders for Pizza Hut have a new deadline this week to submit formal offers. Private equity firms Sycamore Partners, Apollo Global Management (APO), and LongRange Capital are some of the firms reported to be interested in the pizza chain. 

There is a go-private trend in the restaurant sector amid inflation and economic headwinds. "Public [quick service restaurant] stocks are under pressure as softer consumer demand collides with persistent structural cost headwinds," stated EY-Parthenon Strategy Services' Will Auchincloss. "Traffic has weakened as consumers pull back, and at the same time brands are navigating higher labor costs and a far more competitive value environment, he added.

Shares of Papa Johns International (PZZA) were up 4.7% in late morning trading, while Pizza Hut parent Yum! Brands (YUM) slipped 0.4%.

data.title: CoreWeave added as a new long at Hedgeye
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) was upgraded to a long from a short idea at Hedgeye with 40% upside potential. 

"We are upgrading CoreWeave from short to long after one year of maintaining a bearish stance," Hedgeye analyst Felix Wang wrote in a note on Wednesday. "While our fundamental concerns have largely played out - particularly around leverage, cash burn, and execution - the investment case has now shifted materially."

The recently announced partnership with with Anthropic represents a "meaningful turning point," Wang added, providing demand visibility and credibility.

"The Anthropic contract and better financing terms pull forward the path to profitability," Wang wrote. "CoreWeave could reach FCF breakeven earlier than previously modeled."

Separately on Wednesday, CoreWeave (CRWV) landed a $6B deal with Jane Street to provide AI cloud computing services to the global technology-driven trading firm.

data.title: S&P 500 gets close to all-time-high as investors stay optimistic about Middle East developments
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages advanced on Wednesday as investors awaited good news on the U.S.-Iran conflict front while parsing more bank and financial earnings.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is tightly close to its all-time high of 7,002.28 and was last +0.4%.

The heavy-tech Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last +0.8%, aided by Broadcom (AVGO), +4%, as it entered into a three-year partnership with Meta Platforms ((META)) to develop its AI chips and deploy them into data centers.

Nonetheless, the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) was -0.3%.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 3 basis points higher at 4.28%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.77%.

“The 2-year Treasury yield ((US2Y)) fell below 3.75% today (the upper bound of the fed funds rate) for the first time since March 17th as the likelihood of a rate cut by year-end has increased,” said Collin Martin, head of fixed income research and strategy at Schwab Research. “The outlook is highly uncertain, and there's a lot of time between now and the December meeting. For now, we expect the Fed to remain on hold for the next several meetings.”

On geopolitics, President Donald Trump hinted that negotiations with Iran could take place “over the next two days” in Pakistan. This was after the U.S. announced a move towards a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

Reports surfaced that the U.S. and Iran may have made significant progress towards a framework agreement to end the war, according to U.S. officials.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were higher at $92, while Brent (CO1:COM) was at $95 per barrel.

On the economy, U.S. export prices climbed faster than U.S. import prices in March, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. Also, the Empire State Manufacturing Index rose 11 points to 11.0 in April, vs. the -2.0 consensus, the Federal Reserve Bank of New York reported.
